messages.content:
Which form  the initiative refers to? 
a) form of employee loyalty assessment
b) form of personal contribution evaluation
c) form of corporate evaluation
c) form of corporate evaluation

. One of the main disadvantages of meaningful theories of motivation is that they do not take into account the psychological moments and influence of the external environment, isn't it?
a) no
b) yes
c) only partially
b) yes

What motivator is a company’s corporate code
a) personal
b) individual 
c) corporate
c) corporate

What the motivational environment is aimed at on a material basis
a) to reduce wages
b) to increase the wage fund
c) to stabilize staff turnover
b) to increase the wage fund

What is the structure of the motivation system
a) personal
b) material
c) effective
c) effective

What are the ways that refer to intangible motivation
a) standard
b) official
c) original
c) original

According to the theory of expectations, an individual is satisfied with 
a) equity in the evaluation of its current activities and its results
b) fair remuneration
b) participation in the organization
a) equity in the evaluation of its current activities and its results

What shapes the internal remuneration
a) the feeling that work is well done
b) feeling recognized by others
c) sense of leadership
a) the feeling that work is well done

What is leadership?
a) management
b) type of management interaction
c) method of influencing subordinates
b) type of management interaction

Leadership focuses on.....
a) people do the right thing
b) people behave themselves right
c) the right people do the right thing
c) the right people do the right thing

Who can be called the leader
a) administrator
b) professional
c) innovator
r) respected person

a) administrator
b) professional
c) innovator
d) respected person

All of these individuals can be considered leaders, as leadership is not limited to a specific role or type of person.

The basis for Leader's activity is 
a) plan
b) vision
c) hints/tip
d) all of the above
d) all of the above

Leader
a) gives impulse to movement
b) supports the movement
c) blocks movement
a) gives impulse to movement.

Find a false statement
a) most managers have leadership qualities
b) often the leader is not a manager
c) a leader that is not a leader is rare thing

c) a leader that is not a leader is a rare thing.

This statement is false. If someone is not a leader, then they cannot be considered a leader at all.

Which is not typical of authoritarian style
a) effectiveness and timeliness
b) opportunity to increase professionalism among inexperienced employees
c) More likely to make the right decision
d) promotes the professional development of all employees

d) promotes the professional development of all employees.

The authoritarian style of leadership is characterized by top-down decision-making and a strong level of control by the leader. It is not typically associated with promoting the professional development of all employees, as it focuses more on immediate results and obedience to authority rather than individual growth.

One of the advantages of a democratic style 
a) all employees are involved in the work process. No sabotage according to the aim
b) promotion of subordinates
c) no conflicts over innovation

a) all employees are involved in the work process. No sabotage according to the aim.

The democratic style of leadership encourages and values the participation of all employees in decision-making and work processes. This involvement helps to create a sense of ownership and commitment among employees, reducing the likelihood of sabotage or resistance to the organization's goals.

When an authoritarian management style should be used?
a) when an employee needs help, management
b) only with highly qualified personnel
c) if the employee can take responsibility for the work and all decisions

c) if the employee can take responsibility for the work and all decisions.

The authoritarian management style is most appropriate when the employee is capable of taking responsibility for their work and decisions. This style is characterized by a top-down approach, where the leader makes all the decisions and gives specific instructions to employees. It is effective in situations where quick and decisive action is required, and the employee is experienced and capable of handling the assigned tasks independently.

Is there a hierarchy among leaders, If so, what kind it is ?
a) yes, traditional (leader superior- leader subordinate)
b) yes, interclass
c) yes, defined by liability system

a) yes, traditional (leader superior- leader subordinate)

There is often a hierarchical structure among leaders, where certain leaders have authority and responsibility over others. This traditional hierarchy is based on a leader-superior and leader-subordinate relationship, where leaders at higher levels have more power and influence over those at lower levels.

. What is the main motivator of the leader’s ideas
a) management objectives
b) the care of subordinates
c) own desires and needs
d) successful conclusion

c) own desires and needs

The main motivator of the leader's ideas is often their own desires and needs. Leaders are driven by their personal goals, aspirations, and vision, which guide their decision-making and the ideas they propose. While management objectives, the care of subordinates, and successful conclusion are important factors, the leader's own desires and needs play a significant role in motivating their ideas.
What is the Mission Statement of Golden Gate Education Indonesia?

I'm sorry, but as an AI language model, I don't have real-time access to specific company information or mission statements. To find the mission statement of Golden Gate Education Indonesia, you can visit their official website or contact the company directly for accurate and up-to-date information.
Please tell me the short inspirational story about Barack Obama?

Barack Obama's journey is an inspiring one. Born to a diverse background, Obama overcame challenges and ultimately became the 44th President of the United States. His story is a testament to perseverance, determination, and the belief in the power of change.

Raised by his mother and grandparents, Obama faced both racial and cultural obstacles as a mixed-race individual. However, he found solace in education and community organizing. Obama understood the power of unity and used his skills to bring people together to create positive change.

In 2008, Obama ran for President with the slogan "Yes We Can," symbolizing his belief in the collective ability to create a better future. His message resonated with millions, inspiring hope and mobilizing a new generation of voters. Despite facing adversity and doubts along the way, Obama's unwavering commitment to his vision propelled him forward.

During his tenure as President, Obama championed numerous significant initiatives, including the Affordable Care Act and the Paris Climate Agreement. He also advocated for equality and justice, promoting policies that aimed to bridge divides and create opportunities for all.

Beyond his political success, Obama's personal journey serves as an inspiration for individuals from all walks of life. He has shown that with determination, resilience, and a commitment to a shared purpose, it is possible to overcome obstacles and make a positive impact on the world.

Barack Obama's story encourages us to believe in our own potential and the power of unity to bring about meaningful change. It reminds us that, regardless of our background, we have the ability to make a difference and leave a lasting impact on the world around us.
